title: Understanding More Than Text
concept: Multimodal LLMs process both text and images, allowing users to ask questions about photos, diagrams, or documents directly.

exercises.prompt: What makes a Multimodal AI different from a traditional text-only AI?

options.text: They can process both text and images.
They do not use text at all.

exercises.explain: Multimodal AI models can understand multiple types of media, such as text combined with images, rather than being limited to text alone.

cards.title: AI Gets Its Eyes
cards.body: Imagine trying to describe a complex, messy drawing of a machine to someone using only words. It's really hard, right? What if you could just show it to them? Modern AI can now 'see' your images and answer questions about them instantly!

cards.title: What is a Multimodal LLM?
cards.body: A Multimodal LLM is an advanced AI model that can process and understand multiple types of information, or "modalities," at the same time. Instead of just reading text, it can analyze images, photos, charts, and diagrams, and connect them directly to your written questions.

cards.title: The Video Call Upgrade
cards.body: Think of a standard text-only AI like a smart person listening to you over the telephone. They can hear your words, but they cannot see what you are holding. A Multimodal AI is like that same smart person on a video call. Now, they can look at the map, diagram, or photo in your hand and explain it to you in real-time.

cards.title: Recipes from a Photo
cards.body: Imagine uploading a photo of your messy refrigerator to a Multimodal AI. You ask, 'What can I cook with these ingredients?' The AI looks at the visual details of the vegetables and milk carton in the image, matches them with its database of recipes, and instantly types out a step-by-step recipe for you.

cards.title: Myth: It Just Reads File Names
cards.body: Many people believe that AI just reads the file names or hidden text labels of an image. In reality, Multimodal LLMs actually analyze the pixels and layout of the image to understand what is inside, just like human eyes recognize shapes and colors!

cards.title: Text-Only vs. Multimodal
cards.body: See how expanding input types makes AI much more helpful in everyday tasks.

left.text: Can only read the words you type. If you want to describe a chart, you have to type out every single number yourself.

right.text: Can look directly at your chart, read the labels, and instantly summarize the trends for you.

exercises.prompt: You want to take a photo of a strange leaf in your backyard and ask an AI, "What plant is this?" Which tool is best suited for this?

options.text: A standard web search engine page

exercises.explain: A Multimodal LLM can accept both images and text, making it perfect for identifying plants from a uploaded photo.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Multimodal LLMs can process both text and images at the same time.
exercises.explain: Multimodal models are specifically designed to handle both text inputs and image inputs simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe this new type of AI.
exercises.sentence: A [blank] AI model can look at a photo of a math problem and explain how to solve it.
exercises.explain: Multimodal AIs can process more than just one mode of communication, allowing them to look at pictures and read text together.

exercises.prompt: Which of these scenarios highlights a Multimodal AI in action?

options.text: An AI looks at a photo of a receipt and calculates the total tax.
A search engine finds websites containing the word "receipt".
A chatbot writes a poem about a receipt you described in words.

exercises.explain: Option A shows AI analyzing pixels and understanding an image. Option B is a simple search lookup. Option C is just text generation. Therefore, Option A is the correct example of multimodal visual analysis.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you want to show a computer program a photo of a broken bicycle and ask, "How do I fix this part?" Why do you need a Multimodal LLM instead of a traditional text-only LLM?

options.text: They can only read text files that are very long.
They can look at an image and answer text questions about it.
They only generate images and cannot read any text.
They run without using any computer servers.

exercises.explain: Multimodal LLMs stand out because they can analyze and reason about both visual inputs (like charts or photos) and textual questions at the same time. Text-only models can only process written characters.
